instruction:
You are an experienced knowledge engineer modelling a specific domain.
Based on the provided competency questions, produce the ontology source code following the OWL format.
Make it as complete as possible, focusing on concepts, properties, domains and ranges applicable from the competency questions.
Generate the ontology in RDF/XML format.

The base URI of the ontology is: {base_uri}
Note: this base URI already ends with '#'. When constructing any URI you must append the local name DIRECTLY to the base URI, do NOT add another '#'.
Correct:   rdf:resource="{base_uri}Wine"
Incorrect: rdf:resource="{base_uri}#Wine" 
Incorrect: rdf:resource="#Wine" Always use this exact URI when constructing rdf:about or rdf:resource values.
For example, a class named "Wine" must be written as:
  rdf:about="{base_uri}Wine"
and an axiom referencing it must use:
  rdf:resource="{base_uri}Wine"
Never use placeholder text or template syntax in URIs, always use the literal base URI shown above.

For the 'Axiom' field of each entity, provide a raw RDF/XML fragment that will be embedded
directly inside the OWL element. For owl:Class entities use <rdfs:subClassOf>, <owl:disjointWith>,
<owl:equivalentClass>, etc. For properties use <owl:inverseOf>, <rdfs:subPropertyOf>, etc.
The same URI rule applies INSIDE the Axiom field, never write '#LocalName' or '{base_uri}#LocalName'.
Example value for Axiom:
"<rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='{base_uri}RegulatedZone'/>\n  <owl:disjointWith rdf:resource='{base_uri}LowEmissionZone'/>"
If an Axiom is not <rdfs:subClassOf> or <owl:disjointWith>, please leave them as comments.
Follow the instruction as: <!-- Axiom: XXX -->
prompt:
Current ontology (RDF/XML form, may be empty on the first step):
{rdf}

Current competency question to address:
  {cq_id}: {cq_value}

First decide: is this competency question already answerable by the ontology above?
 If yes, output the ontology unchanged (return all existing entities).
 If no, extend the ontology by adding new classes / properties / axioms
  while preserving every existing entity.

If the question involves more than two entities or a combination of
entities and datatypes, introduce a pivot (reification) class to capture
the n-ary relationship correctly. For example, "a user accessed a
resource at a given time" should be modelled with a pivot class such
as UserResourceUsage rather than connecting user directly to both
resource and time.

Output the COMPLETE updated ontology as a list of structured entities.
Do not output raw RDF/XML — fill the structured Answer schema.
Remember: class names use PascalCase only (e.g. "Wine").
